text.title: growth-webinar-delivery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-webinar-delivery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Spending more than 20% of the webinar on the pitch or offer presentation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Audiences register for the educational content. Sensing a bait-and-switch causes early dropoff. The 80/20 rule (80% value, 20% pitch) is the maximum promotional tolerance for a live audience.

failure-mode.description: Sending only a "replay available" email and no follow-up sequence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-email post-webinar follow-up typically converts 1–2% of attendees. The 5-email sequence converts 5–15% by serving different buyer readiness stages on different days.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without urgency, "I'll think about it" means "never". A concrete deadline (72 hours, specific date) converts fence-sitters by making inaction costly.
